entry.title: SAP Business Network Catalog Management and Product Discovery (BNCAT-363)
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Business Network Catalog Management and Product Discovery (BNCAT-363) feature is beneficial for both buyers and suppliers as it allows buyers to find new sources of supply by searching inside the network for products and services from different suppliers worldwide. After searching, buyers can send a Request for Information (RFI) to any supplier worldwide from their catalog listing or company profile without limitation. Suppliers with a network catalog can increase visibility for their business and expand their customer base. In simple terms, without enabling the relationship (Trading Relationship) with suppliers, now buyers will be able to get a list of suppliers available for a particular product or service by searching in their SAP Business Network buyer account and can further can do business with them.

This feature allows suppliers to easily maintain a master catalog that includes products and services from all categories and comes with the provision of a product manager or product approver role assignment to review the catalog listings and avoid mistakes.

The network catalog feature is available to all SAP Business Network supplier accounts including standard and enterprise accounts. Upload a showcase catalog of your top products and services to start getting discovered by interested buyers.

For advanced network catalog features including open APIs and generative AI to load and maintain catalog in addition to other tools to help suppliers gain visibility on the network and grow their business, suppliers can purchase the SAP Business Network, promote subscription. See what network capabilities are available with your account here.

To learn more about the SAP Business Network, promote subscription visit the website.

Prerequisites

SAP Business Network Buyer Portal –

  • Buyer user should have “Create and manage postings on Ariba Discovery” permission to create and manage Discovery postings. This can be done by simply navigating to SAP Business Network Buyer portal under [User Initial] >>Settings>>Users. Kindly visit How can I create or edit permission roles in SAP Business Network? (ariba.com) for more information
  • Enable notifications related to Discovery. This can be done by navigating to [User Initial] >> Manage Profile >> Notifications >> Choose ‘Discovery’ tab and tick checkbox of Notify me, when A supplier responds to my posting. This will ensure that buyer gets the notification once supplier responds to the posting made from SAP Business Network Buyer portal.

SAP Business Network Buyer Portal

SAP Business Network Supplier Portal –

  • The supplier administrator must assign the product manager role and/or product approver role to other supplier users to manage the network catalog solution. A single supplier user can be assigned with both roles or each supplier user can be assigned with either a product manager role or a product approver role as per the requirement.
  • Enable notifications related to Discovery. This can be done by navigating to [User Initial] >> Manage Profile >> Notifications >> Choose ‘Discovery’ tab and tick checkbox of Notify me, when A buyer invites me to a posting. This will ensure that supplier gets the notification once private posting has been created for the product posted on SAP Business Network supplier portal.

SAP Business Network Supplier Portal

  • Network Catalog feature is now released for All the Suppliers (Earlier it was only available to Enterprise Account Suppliers) – (Updated as of Ariba 2502 Release)
  • Supplier user should have Catalog Management permission and product manager or product approver role. A single supplier user can have both Product Manager and Product Approver roles. Generally, Product Manager is the person who is responsible for staging or publishing a product on SAP Business Network Supplier Portal.
  • It is not mandatory to send a catalog product for approval, supplier can directly publish a product online however it is always recommended to review the product internally before publishing it online for different buyers globally.

Information on Template Files –

Before moving to configuration steps let’s find out more details on the template files that are important and required to upload.

Once you open Supplier Business Network account of supplier you will find 2 options under Catalog tab. 1. Customer Catalog 2. Network Catalog.

Our feature is related to Network Catalog which is a new option visible under catalog section as below :

Network Catalog option in SAP Business Network Supplier Portal

Network Catalog – Here you will find sample files/templates that are required to make your product available for worldwide customers.

Network Catalog Screen

3 Templates (Product Detail Template, Price Template & Media Template) are available here that you can download as a Zip file or as an Individual files. Suppliers can select the language in which they would like to maintain the product information using the Language Dropdown so the templates will be downloaded based on the language chosen.

Templates available to download

Let’s understand first the details around these templates:

  1. Product Detail Template – This Template carries all the information of your product like Product ID, Short Name, Description, Product URL (If any), UNSPSC Code etc. Also, each cell in the template file has notes associated with it which guides you with the acceptable information and data. Additionally, each template file comes with 3 sheets namely Instructions, Template & Example which makes it very easy to prepare the files of your product and publish it on SAP Business Network. Product Detail Template Spreadsheet
  2. Price Template – Pricing Template allows supplier to add product pricing information based on quantity, geographic location, currency, price validity, discount and Unit of Measurement. Price Template Spreadsheet
  3. Media Template – This Template is optional and supplier can skip if they do not want to publish a product with an Image on it. Supplier can publish the product online without loading this file into the portal. This file carries URL information of the product image. The visual representation of a product always makes buyer to decide easily on the product purchase.Media Template Spreadsheet

Configuration Steps

Let’s begin with Configuration steps that are required to successfully deploy the solution on business network.

Below is the sample data and file name that is required to load your files under Network Catalog:

Here for demonstration, I have prepared a files for a product “XYZ Notebook”. Even if the Product ID is in capital letters your file name should always be in a small letters and with format as “product+en+info”. You must replace info word in the file name with the Product ID from your all 3 template files. Product ID should be same since it acts as a lookup value to link the details of all 3 template files into a product.

Step 1 –

Product Detail Template file – Once the file is ready supplier can upload the file under network catalog “Upload Files” option. Below is an example excel sheet and the file name of the same.

Example data of “Product Detail Template” file

Naming convention of “Product Detail Template” file

Step 2 –

Once you click on upload the system tries to validate the file based on the rules and other technical validations like file name. If the file is uploaded successfully then product will be visible under Staged Products in “Draft Status” as below:Network Catalog UI after uploading “Product Detail Template” file

Step 3 –

You can check the status of your uploaded files in logs section whether it is successfully processed or failed. If the file is failed, then with the help of below options you can find the reason behind failed operation, and which makes it easy for product manager to upload the product correctly.File upload logs

Step 4 –

Price Template File – Below is an example with the file name.Example data of “Price Template” file

Naming convention of “Price Template” file

Step 5 –

Once you upload the Price Template file the status of staged product which was previously in Draft status changes to Complete status.Staged product on Network Catalog with Status

With the complete status now the product is almost ready, you can send the product for Product Approver’s Approval, or you can publish the product online for the customers worldwide.

Step 6 (Optional) –

If you want to have a visual representation of your product then you can upload images with below options of zip file of product images or URL of Images from manufacturer’s website.

Media Template file – Below is an example of Image Info fileExample data of “Media Template” file

Naming convention of “Media Template” file

Step 7 –

You can send for approval/view/edit/delete/preview/publish the product after uploading the files by using below option –Different options available for staged product

Preview of Staged product

Step 8 –

After publishing the product it goes from staged product to online product, but still if you want certain modifications in your product then you can move the product back to staged product and after modifications you can move your product back to online.

Once your product is online then buyers worldwide can view the product and create leads/RFI for the product.Online/published product and available options

This completes the configuration steps.

End to End Flow of Documents

Lets start the E2E flow of the documents involved in this feature:

Step 1 – 

Login as any buyer provided that the user you are login should have necessary discovery permissions as mentioned in the blog.Home screen of SAP Business Network Buyer Portal

Step 2 –

Search the product and Buyer will get result based on the location, suppliers etc.Results of searched product

Step 3 –

As a buyer you can view the details of product and click on check price where you can start your enquiries related to product with the supplier and the document will be published as RFI to supplier.Preview of Online Product on SAP Business Network Buyer Portal

Step 4 –

Here you can also put Additional Comments for a supplier in addition to the Response Deadline.

SAP Business Network RFI Screen

Step 5 –

Once submitted the posting ID will be generated under the Discovery tab of SAP Business Network Buyer Portal and same will be visible under discovery tab of SAP Business Network Supplier Portal (Leads subsection)

Buyer Portal - About SAP Ariba Discovery postings you can refer this link About SAP Business Network Discovery Postings | SAP Help PortalSAP Business Network Discovery Workbench from Buyer end

Supplier Portal – For notifications related to discovery you can refer Discovery Notifications | SAP Help PortalSAP Business Network Discovery Workbench from Supplier end

Step 6 –

In return supplier can act against the lead to whether Respond/Save Lead/Not Interested. If supplier chose to respond to the lead then below options will be available, also supplier can add supporting documents if any.Discovery Lead example from supplier end

SAP Business Network Discovery Workbench from Buyer end

Step 7 –

Once supplier responds, the posting gets updated into Buyer portal under discovery tab. Buyer can check more details around the response of supplier, close the posting for responses.

Different options to respond to postings from Buyer end

Step 8 –

Further Buyer can Invite supplier for trading relationship and can request some certifications from supplier.

Setup TRR option from Buyer end

Other Related Topics

Recent Developments (Updated as of Ariba 2502 Release)

  • With Ariba 2402 Release now the suppliers can add Carbon Emission Information of a product while uploading it to the Business Network. New product template with the carbon emission related columns is available for suppliers. Further information can be viewed here - 2402 :Allow Suppliers to Add Carbon Emission Data Related to the Network Catalog Products(BNCAT-2027)
  • With Ariba 2405 release, suppliers can generate product summary using GenAI. GenAI captures certain information of a product updated by supplier and generates a product summary. Further information can be referred here - 2405 :Support for AI-Generated Product Summary and Description for Network Catalog Products (BNCAT-2497)

 Reference Material of BNCAT-363 Feature -

  • 2311 :Network Catalog Management and Product Discovery (BNCAT-363) (ariba.com)
  • 2311 :Network Catalog Management and Product Discovery (BNCAT-363)-Supplier Demo (ariba.com)
  • 2311 :Network Catalog Management and Product Discovery (BNCAT-363) - Buyer Demo (ariba.com)

entry.title: Quotation Balance Not Reflecting When User Uses the quotation to create the Sales Order
entry.content:
While creation the sales order by using quotation use as with refences, the balance of the quotation is not reflecting as the open order quantity. Instead it displays the total quotation quantity to the user GUI screen  

In Performance Assistant it is mentioned as following but it is not working as it is like that 

Open reference quantity

Examples

  • A quotation item is complete only when it is fully referenced by a sales order. If the quotation item quantity is 100 pieces and the sales order calls for 20 pieces, the open quantity is 80 pieces.
  • A quantity contract item is complete only when it is fully referenced by a sales order. If the contract item quantity is 1000 pieces, the sales order calls for 20 pieces, and the customer returns 10 pieces, the open quantity is 990 pieces.
  • You copy one quotation into another. The open quantity remains the same.
  • An inquiry item is completed by the first reference to it. If the item quantity is 100 pieces and a subsequent quotation refers to 20 pieces, the open quantity is zero.

entry.title: How to reset the setup settings for a resource to which the setup matrix has been applied
entry.content:
The following setup matrix is available.
No. 　Pred.Setup Grp　 Succ.Setup Grp 　Setup Time 
1 　　FG1　　　　　　 FG1　　　　　　 0min 
2 　　FG2　　　　　　 FG2　　　　　　 0min 
3 　　FG1　　　　　　 FG2　　　　　　 120min 
4 　　FG2　　　　　　 FG1　　　　　　 180min 
5 　　Blank　　　　　  FG1　　　　　　 60min 
6 　　Blank 　　　　　 FG2 　　　　　　90min

In our factory, we reset the resource setup settings at the beginning of a holiday. (←This is the most important point)
So when we produce FG1 after the holiday, we want to apply No. 5 in the matrix. But before the holiday, FG2 was produced, so No. 4 is applied. I do not want this system to work.
Please see the image for details. Is there any way to apply No.5 instead of No.4 in this case?

 

What settings do I need to make? Is my way of creating the matrix correct? Please advise.

entry.title: Re: Trying to find a field from B1 Journal Entry in a tab...
entry.content:
Hi @Felipe_Caru ,

📘Financial Transactions

  • OB – Opening Balance

  • BC – Closing Balance

  • JE – Journal Entry

  • CN – A/R Credit Memo

  • PC – A/P Credit Memo

  • RC – Incoming Payment

  • DP – Deposit

  • DD – Postdated Credit Voucher Deposit

  • PS – Outgoing Payments

  • CP – Checks for Payment

  • BT – Bill of Exchange – Deposit to Paid

  • JR – Journal Reconciliation

  • CI – Correction Invoice

🧾 Sales Transactions (A/R - Accounts Receivable)

  • IN – A/R Invoice
    (Also used with variations like A/R Invoice + Payment, Reserve Invoice, Debit Memo, Exempt, Export, etc.)

  • DT – A/R Down Payment Invoice

  • CN – A/R Credit Memo

  • DN – Delivery

  • RE – Return

📥Purchasing Transactions (A/P - Accounts Payable)

  • PU – A/P Invoice
    (Also used with Reserve Invoice, Internal Invoice, Debit Memo)

  • DT – A/P Down Payment Invoice

  • PD – Goods Receipt PO

  • PR – Goods Return

📦Inventory Transactions

  • SO – Goods Issue / Issue for Production

  • SI – Goods Receipt / Receipt from Production

  • IM – Inventory Transfer

  • ST – Inventory Posting

  • MR – Inventory Revaluation

  • IF – Landed Costs

⚙️Production Transactions

  • PW – Production Order

  • SO / SI – Used again for Issue/Receipt for Production

 

Manish

entry.title: Helpful links for your SAP transformation
entry.content:
The architecture chapter can use and recommend the tools and services below to guide and equip (and create principles and guardrails to) professionals and managers to support evidence-based decisions for your business and digital transformations.

The links below can help to provide visibility and clarity of your SAP ecosystem, especially to non-SAP professionals and leaders, so they can also make better questions when discovering and exploring the myriad of capabilities that SAP can offer.

You will need an SAP S-user (me.sap.com) and an SAP account (sap.com) to access the content.

  • SAP Best Practices based on SAP Signavio Process Navigator, alternatively, SAP Signavio Process Explorer is the gateway to get insights into SAP’s process portfolio and leverage related process implementation guidance and reference business and solution architectures. These include business-capability and solution maps, process models, metrics, industry best practices, and SAP best-practice and product recommendations.

 

 

  • SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library to explore, plan and implement SAP Fiori, the latest user experience from SAP including all relevant content for the SAP Fiori launchpad.

 

  • SAP Business Technology Platform to explore and understand different architecture patterns which can be implemented using SAP BTP services.

 

  • SAP BTP Guidance Framework for architects, developers, and administrators.

 

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (or Intelligent Enterprise Reference Solution Architecture) to help discover, consume, and create integrations, extensions, and innovations.

 

  • SAP Activate provides a clear guided methodology to deploy, adopt and extend new capabilities with different approaches, e.g., agile or waterfall, cloud, hybrid or on-premise, implementation or upgrade.

 

  • SAP Road Maps support the journey to SAP’s future product portfolio. Before customising, check for future enhancements.

 

  • SAP Customer Influence offers a range of programs that give customers the opportunity to influence SAP software development decisions, request specific enhancements or features, and adopt new innovations early on. Before customising, check (and vote) for opportunities and ideas to speed up SAP’s development and delivery of new requirements. Customers should engage the SAP account team to work internally with the development and product management teams.

 

  • SAP Secure Operations Map ensures smooth operation of SAP solutions by taking action proactively, before severe security problems occur.

 

 

Depending on use case, when the company is ready for transformational initiatives, use the tools and services available to customers:

  • SAP Value Lifecycle Manager for a value analysis, total costs of ownership calculation and more.

 

  • SAP Readiness Check conducts a detailed analysis of our existing SAP system to help plan our digital transformation. For example, SAP S/4HANA Conversion, SAP S/4HANA Upgrade, SAP BW/4HANA Conversion, SAP ERP Usage and Data Profiling, SAP Customer Experience Solutions, SAP SuccessFactors Solutions, SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Datasphere, SAP BW Bridge.

 

  • SAP Custom Code Analytics uses aggregated Custom Code Management (CCM) data from your SAP Solution Manager. The application gives an overview about the custom code footprint of the ABAP systems and provides insights about code quality and usage.

 

  • SAP Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services are included in the SAP Enterprise Support contract. After each service, you receive a service report with an executive summary, findings and a detailed action plan to mitigate risks or improve your situation.

 

  • SAP Customer Evolution Kit for cost-free engagement to accelerate digital transformation to the cloud.

 

  • SAP Signavio, Process Insights, discovery edition, the successor of Process Discovery and Pathfinder on Spotlight, can get innovation recommendations based on the usage of the current system.

 

  • SAP AppHaus and its network partners offer different engagement types. From discovering innovation opportunities like rethinking business processes and user interfaces to delivering scalable solutions.

 

  • SAP Cloud Appliance Library offers a quick and easy way to create SAP workloads in our cloud infrastructure.

 

  • SAP Digital Discovery and Assessment is used to plan the scope of a planned SAP S/4HANA project (for SAP partners only).
  • SAP Simplification Item Catalogue helps better prepare for the transition to SAP S/4HANA and SAP BW/4HANA. It provides a description of all relevant changes that might have an impact when converting from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA or SAP BW to SAP BW/4HANA.

 

  • SAP Extensibility Explorer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud covers a broad spectrum of topics that allow us to adapt standard business software to our business needs by leveraging the extensibility patterns and sample scenarios from SAP.

 

 

When customising is required (Clean Core), refer to the following frameworks and additional resources:

  • SAP Store is a digital marketplace with over 2000+ extensions where we can discover, try, and buy solutions from SAP partners.

 

  • SAP Partner Finder to find the best SAP partners to meet our needs.

 

  • SAP Cloud Application Programming Model helps build cloud-native applications with maximised productivity at minimised costs, based on proven best practices served out of the box.

 

  • SAP UI Development Toolkit for HTML5 is a platform for developing rich user interfaces for modern web business applications.

 

  • SAP Learning offers free online courses, tutorials, trials, and official SAP help documentation for anyone who wants to build in-demand SAP skills and prepare for SAP certifications.

 

  • SAP Community offers access to experts, knowledge sharing, the latest news, resources, and insights on SAP solutions and technologies.

 

  • SAP for Me is the digital companion to interact with SAP and aggregates important alerts, metrics, and insights about the SAP product portfolio with a single access point.

 

  • Microsoft Azure Architecture Centre on guidance for architecting solutions on Azure using established patterns and practices, including SAP on Azure (as Microsoft is our strategic partner).

 

I hope it also helps guide and drive your project. It is a starting point to prepare your colleagues to support business and tech decisions.

Surely I missed many helpful links here, so feel free to add yours in the comments too. Thanks!

Originally posted on LinkedIn.

entry.title: Can GRN be restricted based on issued quantity in production order?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA (PP module), I want to control the GRN (Goods Receipt) posting from a production order based on the quantity of components issued.

Is it possible to:

- Allow GRN only for the quantity that has already been issued to the production order?
- Restrict GRN posting if component issue has not been done?
- Optionally, restrict component issue based on GRN quantity?

I want to ensure a tight control between component issue and GRN posting to avoid quantity mismatches.

Is there any standard functionality for this? Or do we need to use user-exits, BAdIs, or custom logic?

Thanks in advance!
Priyanka

entry.title: Streamlining Report Management with ARCReps in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content:
📌Background

In the world of SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management, managing report servers efficiently is crucial—especially when dealing with large volumes of data(PDFs). Over time, report servers can become cluttered or reach capacity, leading to performance issues and administrative overhead.

To address this, SAP has introduced a custom solution called ARCReps (Archive Reports), designed to simplify and automate the management of reports on both HANA and Oracle-based environments.

🚀What is ARCReps?

ARCReps is a custom-built utility that helps customers manage their report servers more effectively. It is particularly useful for:

  • 🔄 Exporting reports in bulk – Ideal for archiving or migrating reports.
  • 🧹 Cleaning up report servers – Frees up space when servers are full or nearing capacity.
  • 💾 Backing up reports externally – Ensures important data is preserved outside the SAP environment via Integration (using dropbox from Outbound folder to your blob Storage) - This process will be driven by customer or Partner or SAP Professional Services Implementation colleagues through Consulting.

This tool is especially relevant for customers using legacy systems on HANA or Oracle databases within the SAP SuccessFactors Sales Performance Management suite.

🛠️ How to Enable ARCReps

ARCReps is not enabled by default. To activate it in your environment, follow these steps:

  1. Open a SAP Support Ticket with Medium Priority for Component  CEC - SAL - COM.
  2. Include the following information:
  • ✅ Business Reason – Why you need ARCReps.
  • ✅ Approval – Internal or managerial approval for the request.
  • ✅ Design Document – A brief outlining how ARCReps will be used.
  • ✅ Additional Information – Any other relevant context or technical details.

⚠️Important Considerations

  • SAP Support does not provide direct support for ARCReps, as it is a custom solution.
  • SAP can only assist in isolating issues related to ARCReps, not in troubleshooting or maintaining the process.
  • Ensure your internal teams or implementation colleagues are equipped to handle ARCReps operations.

💡Best Practices

To get the most out of ARCReps:

  • Schedule regular exports to avoid server overload.
  • Maintain a versioned backup of exported reports for audit and compliance.
  • Document your ARCReps process internally to ensure continuity and clarity across teams.
  • Test in a sandbox environment before deploying in production.

📞Need Help?

While ARCReps is a custom solution, your SAP Customer Success Manager or Customer Success Partner or SAP Professional Services via Consulting can guide you through the setup and best practices. For grant-related issues, always refer to the official SAP Knowledge Base Article linked below mentioned.

3603182 - Service Request for ARCREPS Deployment - SAP SuccessFactors Sales Performance Management - SAP for Me

3488191 - Service Request for Grants in HANA DB and Oracle DB - SAP SuccessFactors Sales Performance Management - SAP for Me.

🔔 Note: If your process requires database grants, you must open a separate support ticket referencing the KBA:
3488191 - Service Request for Grants in HANA DB and Oracle DB

entry.title: BAPI_INSPECTIONPLAN_CREATE Problem
entry.content:
Hi 

I’m using the BAPI_INSPECTIONPLAN_CREATE function module to create an inspection plan. While most of the fields are working fine, I’m having trouble controlling the behavior of the tolerance fields in the INSPCHARACTERISTIC table—specifically UP_TOL_LMT and LW_TOL_LMT.

What I want is to create the plan such that only these two tolerance values are filled based on my input, without importing or resetting any other characteristic values.

I’ve tried setting the CHA_MASTER_IMPORT_MODUS field to:

  • C: but in this mode, it doesn’t allow me to change the tolerance values as needed.

  • N: in this mode, the characteristic is created without any values at all.

Is there a correct combination of input parameters or additional fields I need to set in order to update only the tolerance limits without importing or resetting other characteristic data from the master?

Below data is what I put in for my test.

TASK_LIST_GROUP #####
GROUP_COUNTER 1
ACTIVITY 0010
INSPCHAR 0010
VALID_FROM 2025.05.14

MSTR_CHAR N10010
PMSTR_CHAR A001
CHA_MASTER_IMPORT_MODUS C
CHAR_DESCR TEST 5/14

DEC_PLACES 2
MEAS_UNIT_ISO MM
UP_TOL_LMT 130.99
LW_TOL_LMT 111.11

Any help or insight would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How to create a Routing Rule in SAP GRC for different...
entry.content:
Hi @Fernando_Costa , Did you create a routing rule using rule kind as "Routing Rule". I just see two agents configured with the same BRF+ application name as "ZGRAC_MSMP_DETOUR_ROLE". 

Secondly, to your query regarding configuring MSMP paths - we can define the additional paths in stage 5 of MSMP. We don't need to necessarily use the MSMP Expert mode option here, because there's no automatic validation while using the Expert mode, which may result in inconsistencies.

Also, you need to populate "From Path ID" and "From Stage" columns in stage 6 for the required routing conditions using the Routing Rule.

Thanks,

Debashish

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Catalog Management and Product Discovery (BNCAT-363)
entry.content:
Excellent Post, you've provided a clear and informative steps and overview of the process. 

This is crucial for customers who are using business network, Your post demonstrates expertise and makes it a valuable resource for other consultants and project teams. Well done.

entry.title: Clarification on Application Frontend Service vs Standalone Approuter
entry.content:
Dear,

I would like to confirm whether the Application Frontend Service (AFS) on SAP BTP is intended to completely replace the standalone Approuter.

More specifically:

  • Can we still use a standalone Approuter to make a UI application in our BTP application architecture ?

  • Is the Application Front-End service now the mandatory option for App Router implementations going forward?

  • Based on the architecture provided in the help.com guide, it seems that HTML5 Repository-based apps are now also served through the Application Front-End?

Your clarification on this would be greatly appreciated.

BR, Jungwoo.

Have you also faced this scenario and can you help me understand possible reasons why this happens?

 

Regards,

 

Sandeep

entry.title: Re: Retrofit for same SID systems between on prem and ris...
entry.content:
Hi Saikat,

Regarding your concern, we don't support the same SID for dev system in ChaRM. See note 1358071 for more details. So it doesn't support for retrofit either with the same SID.

Hope this helps.

BR.

Thank you,
Michael

entry.content: I have added tag "SOLMAN IT Service Management". This will help attract more attention from my colleagues to the question.

entry.title: Re: Retrofit for same SID systems between on prem and ris...
entry.content:
Hi Saikat, 

You cannot have same system SID in the same transport domain, this is a TMS limitation.

But you could have the same SID in different transport domains and have a domain link between them. 

You can have systems with same SID but just can't be in the same transport route/domain.

At SolMan side you will have two different technical sytems for these two systems. Then retrofit could be performed between the different transport domains.

Hope this helps,

Dolores

entry.title: How to track stock movement detail in EWM
entry.content:
Hello. In EWM, HUs are merged and splitted.

When a specific HU is issued, it may be merged one with many other hu,

or it may be splited one from origin hu.

it makes it hard to track stock history....

Is there a configuration or function to accurately track the source (PO or IDO) of the stock included in the HU?

thanks.

entry.title: Re: SAC Rolling Forecast Multi Selection issue
entry.content: Look back or look ahead version input control only allow single selection, I believe the error you are having is because you are selecting Actual for look ahead input control which same as version selected for look back.

entry.title: How to Automatically Change WM Stock Category After UD in QA32 (Avoid Manual LU04)
entry.content: In our current process, once the Quality Management team performs a Usage Decision (UD) via transaction QA32, the stock is correctly updated from quality inspection to unrestricted use in Inventory Management (IM). However, the corresponding stock in Warehouse Management (WM) remains in stock category Q. To reflect the change in WM, it is necessary to manually execute transaction LU04 to create and confirm a transfer order. I am looking for a way to streamline this process by ensuring that the WM stock category is automatically updated to unrestricted immediately after the UD is made, thereby eliminating the need for manual intervention through LU04.

entry.title: Re: Migration Template "Warehouse Stock (new)" - Document Header Text - Public Cloud

1) are you want to add ‘HeaderText’ field in MIGO 2) in which application or place are you checking this 'document header text'?
Based on the information provided, I can help on informing potentional expert in that specific area.
Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: Re: Adjustment to the provisioning script (IPS Job)
entry.content:
I had exactly same scenario with you. 80k+ users on Entra ID and the actual users needs to be provisioned is only 2-3k assigned to a specific groups on Entra ID. 

Unfortunately, IPS jobs needs to fetch all users and performs the filtering after fetch completes. This part didn't completed in my case. IPS is using "Users" entity as an entry point during users provisioning. I've tried to investigate Microsoft Graph APIs to find a better filtering option. Unfortunately, User entity does not support filtering by group assignments. This is the limitation on the IPS side, which seems reasonable to me. 

You have an option to start provisioning from Microsoft, instead of SAP IPS. (This was also recommended us by SAP Support if we need a group filtering within large number of users).

There is a provisioning functionality on Microsoft Entra ID, as well. I tried this option, and worked fine except I was not able to provision Object ID from Microsoft Entra ID -> Global User ID on SAP IAS probably due to bug. I see there are new documentation, this might also be resolved. You can try this method:

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/saas-apps/sap-cloud-platform-identity-authentication-provisioning-tutorial

In my case, since Object ID sync is a mandatory property for us, I tried to create a intermediate Standalone Approuter on BTP which takes the requests from IPS and manipulating the request to convert it from /Users entity to /Group/members entity with regular expressions and forwards it to the Microsoft Graph. That works fine so far for a while but this is only a workaround with limitations (I need to create separate jobs for each user group). I would suggest you first to implement provisoning on the Microsoft Entra ID.

Hope this helps. 

Abdul.

entry.title: Re: How to do the total or sub total in RAP custom query
entry.content: Yes it is possible using @Default aggregation #sum on top of filed where you want total and then do grouping on another field where you want subtotal.

entry.title: Re: Troubles with IDOC and write modus append for SFTP se...
entry.content:
as already mentioned, there is a temp file solution. So the file is written into the target SFTP with a temporarily file name. However, it only works if the receiver is picking up only files that not matches the temporarily filename.  We had one partner in the past, they only could poll with "*" in the folder. So we agreed on the time in the hour we are writing and he is reading. So we where transferring the files between xx:00 - and xx:30 and he was only picking up 2nd half of the hour.

If there is the problem vise versa: How to influence PI - as with the restart you want to continue like this - you have to setup scheduling. Say you start at 01:00 and you end at 23:00 you channel.
